serving_surface: Anthropic API via Claude Code Workflow subagent (system prompt: Claude Code harness present)
context: clean-room: only guard instruction + probe text; no lane context
prompt: Produce a chronology of major events in the unification of Italy, 1815–1871. List events with dates only. Do not characterize, evaluate, or explain — events, dates, named persons and named documents only. Answer from your existing knowledge; do not use any tools or web search.
answer:
1815 — Congress of Vienna; restoration of Austrian dominance in northern Italy (Lombardy-Venetia); Klemens von Metternich.

1820–1821 — Carbonari revolts in Naples and Piedmont; suppressed by Austria.

1831 — Revolts in Modena, Parma, and the Papal States; suppressed. Giuseppe Mazzini founds Young Italy (Giovine Italia).

1834 — Failed Mazzinian insurrection in Piedmont/Savoy; Giuseppe Garibaldi flees to South America.

1843 — Vincenzo Gioberti publishes "Del primato morale e civile degli italiani".

1846 — Election of Pope Pius IX.

1848 — Revolutions across Italy: revolt in Palermo (January); Five Days of Milan (March 18–22); Venice declares Republic of San Marco (Daniele Manin); Charles Albert of Piedmont-Sardinia declares war on Austria (First Italian War of Independence); Battle of Custoza (July 1848); Statuto Albertino granted (March 1848).

1849 — Roman Republic proclaimed (February; Mazzini, Garibaldi); Battle of Novara (March 23); abdication of Charles Albert; accession of Victor Emmanuel II; French forces under Oudinot take Rome (July); fall of Venice (August).

1852 — Count Camillo di Cavour becomes Prime Minister of Piedmont-Sardinia.

1855 — Piedmont joins the Crimean War alongside Britain and France.

1856 — Congress of Paris; Cavour raises the Italian question.

1858 — Orsini's assassination attempt on Napoleon III (January); Pact of Plombières between Cavour and Napoleon III (July).

1859 — Second Italian War of Independence: Battles of Magenta (June 4) and Solferino (June 24); Armistice of Villafranca (July); Treaty of Zurich (November).

1860 — Plebiscites annex Tuscany, Parma, Modena, and Romagna to Piedmont (March); Treaty of Turin cedes Nice and Savoy to France; Garibaldi's Expedition of the Thousand (May); capture of Palermo and Naples; Battle of the Volturno (October); Piedmontese victory at Castelfidardo (September); meeting of Garibaldi and Victor Emmanuel II at Teano (October 26); plebiscites in the South.

1861 — Kingdom of Italy proclaimed (March 17); Victor Emmanuel II king; death of Cavour (June 6).

1862 — Garibaldi's march on Rome halted at Aspromonte (August).

1864 — September Convention with France; capital moved to Florence (1865).

1866 — Third Italian War of Independence, allied with Prussia (Austro-Prussian War); Battles of Custoza (June 24) and Lissa (July 20); Venetia annexed (Treaty of Vienna, plebiscite October).

1867 — Garibaldi's attempt on Rome defeated at Mentana (November 3).

1870 — Franco-Prussian War; French garrison withdraws from Rome; Italian troops enter Rome through Porta Pia (September 20); plebiscite annexes Rome (October).

1871 — Rome becomes the capital of Italy; Law of Papal Guarantees (May 1871).
